Rates & current densities

All C-rates defined at the cell level (limiting electrode):
Assign rate to:

Capacity balance

Capacity ratio Qa/Qc (N/P) — at all rates

Potential windows

Voltage range each electrode sweeps, plotted vs. its OCV reference. The dashed outline is the theoretical window Vth taken from the library (the material's full-capacity limits). The solid fill is the operating window Vop the user has set. For capacitive / pseudocapacitive materials the accessible capacity is scaled by Vop/Vth; faradaic capacities are not scaled.

Galvanostatic simulation

1st cycle: no row selected Nth cycle: no row selected
Plot template ready — physics not wired yet. Pick a 1st-cycle and Nth-cycle rate in the Rates & current densities tab to anchor the simulation currents.
Run a calculation first

Export results

Stack mode. Define one or more electrode materials (cathode or anode) — each becomes a tab. Add several mass entries per material; the tool pairs each electrode with the best-matching opposite-side electrode and ranks them by how close their N/P ratio gets to your target. If a side has no real electrodes yet, an ideal partner mass is computed from the other tab's material so a pair is always shown.
N/P

Add new electrode materialcreates a new tab — one tab per composition

No materials yet. Pick a side (cathode/anode) and a library preset (or leave blank for a custom material), then click + Add tab.

MUSIC Project · kc=1 (VW scaling at input level) · unit-aware rates · v9.0Funded by the European Union. Views and opinions expressed are those of the author(s) only and do not necessarily reflect those of the European Union.

Add material

V
References
A reference can carry a figure (e.g. a rate-capacity plot), a manual legend, and a Nature-style citation. Multiple capacity entries can share one reference — pick it from the dropdown on each entry below.
Capacity entries (capacity at a given rate)
Each entry is a capacity (C₁, Cₙ) measured at a specific rate, with its own voltage window and mechanism. Pick a shared reference from above (or "no reference"). Leave a value empty if the source paper doesn't report it.

Manage the embedded benchmark library. Add a new material (with rate-paired C₁/Cₙ, V-window, electrolyte and Nature-style citation); Save file writes the current library back into a downloadable copy of the HTML so it travels with the file; Export/Import JSON moves the library between files; Reset restores the bundled defaults.
